title: قرار رقم 53: بشأن إعتماد الإصدار رقم (1,0) من التوجيهات الخاصة بوضع سقف لاشتراكات الطرف الثالث

provisions.title: يُعملبهذاالقرارمن تاريخصدوره،ويُنشر فيالجريدةالرسمية.
provisions.content: يُعملبهذاالقرارمن تاريخصدوره،ويُنشر فيالجريدةالرسمية.صدربتاريخ24سبتمبر2019م.طلالحميدبالهولرئيسمجلسالإدارةنشرهذا القرار فيعدد الجريدةالرسمية رقم664ص69.Directive(4) of 2019Regardingthe Mandatory Introduction of a CAP Limit on the purchase of AdditionalServicesVersion:1.0IssueDate: 24 Sep. 20191-DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION1.1The terms, words, and phrases used in this Directive shall have the same meaningas are ascribed to them in the Telecommunications Law, unless this Directiveexpressly provides otherwise, or the context in which those terms, words andphrases are used in this Directive requires otherwise. For the purposes of thisDirective, the following terms and words shall have the meanings ascribed tothembelow:1.1.1“ AdditionalService” as defined in the CPR means - any optionalnon-telecommunications service supplied by a Partner or a Licensee including butnot limited to: games, content, apps, ringtones, third-party products,third-party services, etc. and where the fees, levies, or charges for such extraservice(s) are collected by the concerned Licensee via the Subscriber’saccount1.1.2“ AdditionalService Purchase” means - any purchase of an Additional Servicemade by a Consumer, over and above contracted telecommunications service(s),where the purchase price for such purchase is collected by a Licensee through apost-paid invoice, or by a deduction from a Subscriber’s pre-paid creditbalance. See section 5.1 of this Directive for clarifications andexemptions.1.1.3“CAP” means - a Subscriberselectable upper limit on the total amount of money, measured in Dirhams (AED),which can be invoiced orbilledby a Licensee in relation to any Additional Services Purchases made in any onemonth.1.1.4“Consumer Protection Regulations”or “CPR” means - theConsumer Protection Regulations, Version 1.4, Issued 20 March 2019.1.1.5“EITC” means - EmiratesIntegrated Telecommunications Company and its successors.1.1.6“EITC Licence” means -Public Telecommunications Licence No. (2) of 2006 as granted to EITC.1.1.7“Etisalat” means - EtisalatTelecommunications Group and its successors.1.1.8“Etisalat Licence” means -Public Telecommunications Licence No. (1) of 2006 as granted to Etisalat.1.1.9“Executive Order” - meansDecision of the Supreme Committee for the Supervision of the TelecommunicationsSector No. (3) of 2004 Issuing the Executive Order of Federal Law by Decrees No(3) of the 2003 Regarding the Organisation of the TelecommunicationsSector.1.1.10“Extended MandatoryInstructions” - means the letters sent on 30 April 2017 to each ofEITC and Etisalat under the subject of ‘Extended Mandatory InstructionsRegarding Subscriptions to Third Party Applications” and having thereference number TRA/RA/17/1265 for EITC, and TRA/RA/17/1255 for Etisalat.1.1.11“Implementation Date” means- the date, as specified in Article 8.2, by which Licensees shall fullyimplement this Directive.1.1.12“Licensee” means - Etisalat or EITC as appropriate andincludes all ‘brands’ used by Etisalat to provide service under theEtisalat Licence and all 'brands’ used by EITC to provide services underthe EITC Licence.1.1.13“Mandatory Instructions” -means the letters sent on 1 February 2017 to each of EITC and Etisalat under thesubject of “Mandatory Instructions Regarding Subscriptions to Third PartyApplications” and having the reference number TRA/RA/17/1084 for EITC, andTRA/RA/17/1076 for Etisalat.1.1.14“OTP” or“One-time Password” - meansan automatically generated numeric or alphanumeric string of characters that hasa limited-life and authenticates the user for a single transaction or sessiononly.1.1.15“Regulatory Framework”means - the complete body of resolutions, licences, authorisations,policies, regulations, instructions, directives (including this Directive),orders, determinations, reports, procedures, guidelines, and other suchregulatory instruments as issued and as may be issued by the TRA and as may beamended from time-to-time.1.1.16“Telecommunications Law” -means Federal Law by Decree No.3 of 2003 Regarding the Organisation of theTelecommunications Sector, as amended.1.1.17“TRA”or “Telecommunications RegulatoryAuthority” - means the General Authority for Regulating theTelecommunications Sector as established by Article 6 of the TelecommunicationsLaw.1.2In interpreting the provisions of this Directive, unless this Directiveexpresslyprovidesotherwise, or the context requires otherwise:1.2.1words importing one gender shall be construed as importing allgenders;1.2.2words importing the singular shall be construed as importing the plural andvice-versa;1.2.3references to articles, sub-articles, annexure, and attachments shall beconstrued as being references to articles, sub-articles, annexure, andattachments in or to this Directive;1.2.4headings and sub-headings in this Directive are intended for guidance and shallnot be construed as limiting the effect of the provisions of thisDirective.1.3This Directive may be referred to by the short name- the“ CAP LimitDirective ”.2-INTRODUCTION2.1It has become common for Consumers to make Additional Service Purchases viatheir mobile phones or/and other devices, such as TV remote control units. SuchAdditional Purchases usually relate to third-party Apps, ringtones, games,music, videos, in-App purchases, etc. but can equally be related to Licenseesupplied Apps, ringtones, games, music, videos, in-App purchases, etc.2.2The wide proliferation of smartphones, smart -TVs, tablets, computers, on-linegaming consoles etc., has made it very easy for Consumers to make AdditionalService Purchases. In fact, at one point it was so easy for Consumers to makeAdditional Service Purchases it was possible for Consumers to make suchpurchases by accident. Naturally, those circumstances gave rise to a significantvolume of complaints by Consumers regarding disputed purchases and/or excessiveinvoice amounts.2.3Acting in the interests of Consumers, with a view to promoting Consumer welfareby eradicating the cause of such complaints, the TRA has intervened on severaloccasions. The TRA’s interventions have included: amendments to theConsumer Protection Regulations, issuing the Mandatory Instructions, and issuingthe Extended Mandatory Instructions.2.4Despite the TRA’s significant interventions and efforts, the TRA continuesto receive complaints regarding bill shock arising from excessive invoices. TheTRA is greatly alarmed by this situation. Accordingly, the TRA considers thatfurther intervention is required in order to safeguard the interests ofConsumers and to promote customer happiness and welfare and to strengthenConsumer trust in the telecommunications sector. To that end, the TRA is issuingthis Directive to obligate Licensees to implement a monthly CAP limit onAdditional Service Purchases as set out herein.3-LEGAL BACKGROUND3.1Article 12 of the Telecommunications Law establishes the TRA as the competentbody to oversee the telecommunications sector in the State and all Licensees inaccordance with the Telecommunications Law, the Executive Order, and theinstructions of the Board.3.2Article 13(2) of the Telecommunications Law provides that the TRA “shallexercise its functions and powers... to... enhance the level of service providedby the telecommunications sector in order to promote the interests ofsubscribers”.3.3Article 14(3) of the Telecommunications Law grants the TRA with the powers to“issue regulations, instructions, decisions and rules regulating... theconditions, level and scope of services provided by the Licensees tosubscribers... including the... terms of supply”.3.4EITC and Etisalat each provide Public Telecommunications Services in the UAEpursuant to the provisions of the EITC Licence and the Etisalat Licencerespectively. Article 8.2 of each of the EITC Licence and the Etisalat Licencestates: “The Licensee shall comply with any directions as the TRA or othercompetent authority may issue from time to time on matters relating to publicinterest, safety and/or national security...”.3.5The TRA submits that it is in the public interest for Consumers to be protectedagainst accidental Additional Purchases and/or related bill shock.4-THE DIRECTIVE4.1Havingconsidered:(a)the provisions of the Telecommunications Law, it’s Executive Order and theRegulatory Framework; and(b)acting in the interests of protecting the welfare of Consumers; the TRA issuesthis Directive to all Licensees.4.2By no later than the Implementation Date, Licensees shall:4.2.1adopt and implement robust business processes to ensure full compliance withthis Directive;4.2.2inform all Subscribers of the operation of the CAP and advise Subscribers of theprocedure by which Subscribers may select and set a CAP at an amount of theSubscriber’s choosing;4.2.3impose and strictly enforce a CAP on the purchase of Additional Services. TheCAP shall, by default, be set at AED 0 per month which means that only thoseSubscribers who take specific actions in accordance with this Directive will beable to make Additional Services Purchases.4.2.4Develop and implement a mechanism whereby each Subscriber can either select apersonalCAP ,or if the Subscriber prefers to set the CAP as being unlimited.5-SCOPE5.1This Directive applies to all Additional Service Purchases- that is allpurchases and/or subscriptions made on top off of contracted telecommunicationsservices, where the fees related to such purchases are collected by a Licenseeeither as a listed item on a post-paid account, or by deduction of funds from apre-paid credit balance regardless of whether the post-paid or pre-paid accountrelates to mobile or fixed telecommunications services, and regardless of thedevice or devices used by the Consumer to make the Additional Purchase, andregardless of whether the Additional Services are supplied by Licensees or bythird-parties.5.2For the avoidance of doubt, this Directive does not apply to any purchase wherethe fees related to the purchase in question are contemporaneously settled bysome arrangement outside of the Licensee’s billing procedures such as bydirect payment made at the time of the purchase.6-LICENSEE APPs6.1Licensees areencouragedto develop smart functionality on Licensee’s portals including Apps andwebsites etc. whereby Subscribers can use the portals to:6.1.1Individually manage their CAP limits, in order to set, increase, and/or decreasethe monetary amount of their CAP limits;6.1.2track Additional Services Purchases and view the history of such purchases, etc.etc.7-RESOLUTION OF CONSUMER DISPUTES7.1If a Consumer has a complaint regarding an excessive amount being invoiced(post-paid account) or billed (pre-paid account) in relation to AdditionalServices Purchases, the TRA will expect the concerned Licensee to provideevidence that the Subscriber has adjusted his/her default CAP and has set anindividual CAP at a monetary amount higher than that of the disputed invoicedamount for all Additional Services Purchases in the month in question.7.2In the event that the concerned Licensee cannot produce satisfactory evidence asdescribed in 7.1 above, the TRA will instruct that Licensee to reimburse theSubscriber for the whole of the disputed amount.8-EFFECTIVE DATE AND IMPLEMENTATION DATE8.1This Directive shall become effective as of the date of issue.8.2The Implementation Date shall be 1 November2019 .8.3This Directive shall remain in force until otherwise determined by theTRA.9-ACKNOWLEDGEMENT AND COMPLIANCE9.1The Licensees shall notify the TRA in writing of receipt of this Directivewithin one (1) business day of the date it is received.---End of Directive ---[1]The language “invoiced or billed” is intended to cover amountsinvoiced by Licensees through post-paid invoices, as well as amounts deducted byLicensees from pre-paid credit balances. Thus, the CAP applies to pre-paid andpost-paid accounts.[2]In circumstances where Licensees allow Subscribers to select a CAP from a listof predetermined amounts, the extent of the list shall be sufficiently broad toallow ‘low volumeusers’
